creative_brief: Hero image: aerial split-screen — one side showing degraded conventional farmland (dry, brown), the other showing lush regenerative pasture with visible soil life. Below the fold, warm photography of real partner farms (Rachel's Farm, Bundarra, Collins Wild Caught) with farmers' hands in frame. Earthy palette — deep greens, warm browns, cream. No stock photography. Typography is clean, grounded, sentence case throughout.
headline: Your grocery run could regenerate Australian farmland
subhead: FarmThru is a farm-direct grocery store where every order supports regenerative farming, fair prices for farmers, and food you can actually trace. Now you can help build it.
hero_copy:
Right now, most food travels over 2,400km through cold storage, wholesalers, and distribution centres before it reaches you. Farmers get squeezed. Soil gets depleted. And you get produce that's been off the ground for weeks.

FarmThru cuts all of that. No middlemen. No wholesalers. Just regenerative farms connected directly to families across Sydney — through our Brookvale hub and local delivery.

We're opening an equity crowdfunding campaign so you can own a piece of the grocery store that's trying to fix this.

sections.heading: This isn't about shopping differently — it's about what your money builds
sections.body:
Every dollar you spend at a supermarket reinforces a system that degrades soil, underpays farmers, and ships food thousands of kilometres.

Every dollar through FarmThru does the opposite.

Rachel Ward — actor, regenerative farming advocate, and founder of Rachel's Farm in Kempsey, NSW — supplies FarmThru because she's seen what happens when farmers get a direct line to families. Paris Creek Farms in South Australia produces carbon-neutral bio-dynamic dairy. Collins Wild Caught brings seasonal seafood straight from the South Coast of NSW. Bundarra Farm supplies pork from animals raised right.

These aren't labels on a shelf. They're real people farming regeneratively — and FarmThru exists so their work actually reaches your kitchen.

sections.heading: How it works: simpler than you think
sections.body:
Order online from our full regenerative grocery range — not a box, not a subscription. You choose exactly what your family needs, when you need it. No lock-in. No fees.

Collect from our Brookvale hub (Unit 23, 10-18 Orchard Rd) Monday to Friday, or get it delivered across Sydney, the Central Coast, or Wollongong.

Because we skip the big chains' supply chain entirely — zero warehouses, zero wholesalers — your food is fresher and farmers keep more of what they earn. Bulk buying options mean you save too.

We'll show you where your food comes from. Every product. Every farm. Every time.

sections.heading: Be a partner in something that matters
sections.body:
We're preparing an equity crowdfunding campaign — a chance for everyday people to own a piece of FarmThru.

This isn't a donation. It's not a gift card. It's real ownership in a farm-direct grocery store built on the belief that food should regenerate land, not destroy it.

We can't promise returns. There's no guarantee this will be easy. Building an alternative to the biggest chains in Australia is genuinely hard. But if you believe the way we buy food should change — not just for your family, but for the soil, the farmers, and the next generation — this is your chance to vote for that change with your wallet.

Join the waitlist now with a refundable $5 deposit to be first in line when the campaign opens. You'll get early access, founder updates, and a seat at the table before anyone else.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: This is a pre-registration page for an upcoming equity crowdfunding campaign. No money is being raised at this stage. Any future offer will be made via a disclosure document on Birchal. This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice before making any investment decision.
